description: Canonical parameter dictionary for operational amplifiers, synthesized from a 20-datasheet corpus spanning general-purpose/audio (TL072, NE5532, LM358), precision and zero-drift (OPA277, OPA189, ADA4522), JFET/CMOS (LF356, OPA627, OPA827, OPA320, TLV9062, MCP6002), ultra-low-noise (LT1028, AD797, LMH6629), high-speed voltage- and current-feedback (OPA855, THS3491), and femtoampere electrometer (LMP7721, ADA4530-1) parts across TI, Analog Devices and Microchip. Covers voltage-feedback and current-feedback topologies; current-feedback parts have asymmetric inverting/non-inverting input specs and a transimpedance (ohm) open-loop gain. Condition axes for this family: V_S, V_CM, R_L, R_S, C_L, A_V, F, T_A, T_J, plus R_F/R_G for current-feedback parts. Pin functions: IN_P/IN_N (inputs), VS_POS/VS_NEG (dual supply), NULL (offset trim), COMP (compensation), PD (power-down), GUARD (electrometer guard buffer). Two modeling conventions the corpus made universal: input impedance printed as 'R parallel C' is split into an input_resistance and an input_capacitance parameter; a rail-relative spec (common-mode range, output swing 'from the rail') is stored as the headroom magnitude from the rail with the printed form in conditionsVerbatim. Set component.polarity to bipolar for a dual-supply part. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Input offset voltage
parameters.hint: Differential input offset, at T_A = 25 degC unless a temperature range is given. Often specified as a +/- magnitude; store as min = -X, max = +X. Grade suffixes set this number; use one document per grade.

parameters.name: Input offset voltage drift
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the input offset voltage. The zero-drift/chopper parts (OPA189, ADA4522) put their headline here (a few nV/degC).

parameters.name: Input offset current
parameters.hint: Difference of the two input bias currents. Often array over T_A. Symmetric +/- values map to min/max.

parameters.name: Input offset current drift
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the input offset current (the current analog of V/degC).

parameters.name: Long-term offset drift
parameters.hint: Offset-voltage change over an elapsed time. Datasheets print uV/1000 hr or uV/month; store the absolute change in volts and keep the rate and duration in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Offset temperature hysteresis
parameters.hint: Residual offset shift after a temperature cycle back to 25 degC.

parameters.name: Input bias current
parameters.hint: Average input bias current, strongly temperature-dependent for JFET/CMOS parts, so one measurement per T_A point. Femtoampere on electrometer parts. For a current-feedback amplifier the two inputs differ; use input_bias_current_inverting/_noninverting instead.

parameters.name: Input bias current drift
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the input bias current (common on high-speed bipolar parts).

parameters.name: Non-inverting input bias current
parameters.hint: Current-feedback amplifiers only: the (buffer) non-inverting input bias current, distinct from the inverting input.

parameters.name: Inverting input bias current
parameters.hint: Current-feedback amplifiers only: the inverting (feedback-node) input bias current.

parameters.name: Common-mode input voltage range
parameters.hint: The input common-mode range over which the amplifier is specified. Frequently rail-relative (e.g. (V-)+2 to (V+)-2); store the headroom from each rail and preserve the printed form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Common-mode rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Rejection of a common-mode input, tested over a stated V_CM range. Usually dB; some vendors give it as a uV/V ratio (V/V).

parameters.name: Power-supply rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Rejection of a supply change. dB for most parts; precision/zero-drift parts (OPA189) often spec it as an offset shift in uV/V (V/V). Split PSRR+ and PSRR- into two measurements with a DIRECTION note when given separately.

parameters.name: Differential input resistance
parameters.hint: Resistance between the two inputs. When printed as 'R parallel C', store the R here and the C in input_capacitance_differential.

parameters.name: Common-mode input resistance
parameters.hint: Resistance from either input to the supplies. Over 100 TOhm on electrometer parts. Split from any parallel capacitance.

parameters.name: Differential input capacitance
parameters.hint: Capacitance between the inputs; the C of a printed 'R parallel C' input-impedance cell.

parameters.name: Common-mode input capacitance
parameters.hint: Capacitance from either input to the supplies.

parameters.name: Open-loop voltage gain
parameters.hint: Large-signal DC open-loop gain, swept over load resistance and output-swing range. dB or the dimensionless V/V (a V/mV or V/uV figure normalizes to V/V). For a current-feedback amplifier the open-loop gain is a transimpedance in ohms; use transimpedance_gain instead.

parameters.name: Open-loop transimpedance gain
parameters.hint: Current-feedback amplifiers only: the open-loop transimpedance (output voltage per inverting-input error current), in ohms (megohms). Replaces open_loop_gain for a CFA.

parameters.name: Gain-bandwidth product
parameters.hint: Gain-bandwidth product (voltage-feedback parts). Note the gain the amplifier must be stable at (A_V) in a condition, since decompensated parts (LT1028, OPA855) only specify it at a minimum gain.

parameters.name: Slew rate
parameters.hint: Output slew rate at a stated gain and step. Rising and falling can differ; use two measurements with a DIRECTION note when given separately.

parameters.name: Phase margin
parameters.hint: Phase margin at unity gain (or the minimum stable gain), in degrees. Note the load capacitance C_L in a condition.

parameters.name: Small-signal bandwidth
parameters.hint: Small-signal -3 dB bandwidth at a stated closed-loop gain and small output amplitude. Common on high-speed parts.

parameters.name: Large-signal / full-power bandwidth
parameters.hint: Bandwidth at a large (full-scale) output swing; slew-rate limited. Note the output amplitude.

parameters.name: Settling time
parameters.hint: Time to settle within a stated error band (0.1 %, 0.01 %, 16-bit) after a step. One measurement per error band; note the step size and gain.

parameters.name: Overload recovery time
parameters.hint: Time to recover from an input overdrive that saturates the output.

parameters.name: Rise / fall time
parameters.hint: 10-90 % edge time for a small step (high-speed parts). Rising/falling separable via a DIRECTION note.

parameters.name: Input voltage noise (band)
parameters.hint: Integrated input-referred voltage noise over a bandwidth (usually 0.1 Hz to 10 Hz peak-to-peak, or a wideband RMS figure). Set the statistic field to peak_to_peak or rms and give BW_LOW/BW_HIGH.

parameters.name: Input voltage noise density
parameters.hint: Input-referred voltage noise spectral density; one measurement per frequency. The 1/f region and the flat (broadband) floor are separate points on the same key.

parameters.name: Input current noise density
parameters.hint: Input-referred current noise spectral density (A/sqrtHz), the current analog of en. For a current-feedback amplifier the inverting and non-inverting inputs differ; capture both with a note.

parameters.name: Total harmonic distortion (+ noise)
parameters.hint: Harmonic distortion, per frequency, at a stated output amplitude, gain and load. Audio parts give it in % or dB; high-speed parts give HD2/HD3 relative to the carrier (store as dB, note 'dBc' in conditionsVerbatim).

parameters.name: Second-harmonic distortion
parameters.hint: High-speed parts: 2nd-harmonic distortion relative to the carrier (dBc), per frequency and output amplitude.

parameters.name: Third-harmonic distortion
parameters.hint: High-speed parts: 3rd-harmonic distortion relative to the carrier (dBc), per frequency and output amplitude.

parameters.name: Intermodulation distortion
parameters.hint: Two-tone intermodulation distortion, per center frequency; note the tone spacing and amplitudes.

parameters.name: Channel separation (crosstalk)
parameters.hint: Multi-amplifier parts: isolation between channels, per frequency.

parameters.name: EMI rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Rejection of RF interference at an input, per frequency; note the RF power level.

parameters.name: Output voltage swing
parameters.hint: Maximum output swing, swept over load resistance. For a rail-to-rail part this is the headroom from each rail (e.g. 25 mV from V+), not an absolute voltage; store the headroom magnitude and preserve the printed rail-relative form in conditionsVerbatim, with a DIRECTION note for the high vs low rail.

parameters.name: Output short-circuit current
parameters.hint: Current into an output short. Source and sink can differ; use one measurement per direction with a DIRECTION note.

parameters.name: Output current
parameters.hint: Specified linear output drive current (distinct from the short-circuit current). High-power parts (THS3491) spec several hundred mA.

parameters.name: Open-loop output impedance
parameters.hint: Output impedance at a stated frequency; open-loop or closed-loop as noted.

parameters.name: Capacitive load drive
parameters.hint: The capacitive load the amplifier drives with a stated overshoot/phase margin.

parameters.name: Supply voltage
parameters.hint: The total supply voltage (V+ minus V-) range: recommended for the operating range, absolute_max for the stress limit. A dual-supply part states it as a +/- magnitude.

parameters.name: Quiescent current
parameters.hint: Supply current with no load, per amplifier (note when the datasheet gives it per channel vs total). Often array over T_A.

parameters.name: Shutdown supply current
parameters.hint: Supply current when the amplifier is disabled via its power-down pin.

parameters.name: Enable / power-down threshold voltage
parameters.hint: Logic thresholds of the enable/power-down (PD) pin. Use min for the guaranteed-on level and max for the guaranteed-off level, or one measurement per level.

parameters.name: Turn-on / turn-off time
parameters.hint: Enable/disable delay of the power-down pin; use a DIRECTION note for on vs off.

parameters.name: Guard-buffer offset voltage
parameters.hint: Electrometer parts with an integrated guard buffer (ADA4530-1): the guard-output-minus-input offset. The guard buffer is a specified second amplifier; group its parameters under guard_buffer.

parameters.name: Guard-buffer output impedance
parameters.hint: Output impedance of the integrated guard buffer.

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Per-package junction-to-ambient thermal resistance.

parameters.name: Junction-to-case thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Per-package junction-to-case thermal resistance (top).

parameters.name: Power dissipation
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum internal power dissipation, package-dependent.

parameters.name: Input voltage (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum voltage at an input. Frequently rail-relative ((V-)-0.5 to (V+)+0.5); keep the printed form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Differential input voltage (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum voltage between the two inputs; small on parts with input clamp diodes.

parameters.name: Input current (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum current into an input pin (clamp-diode limit).

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: Specified operating temperature range (recommended), or the wider absolute-maximum operating range.

parameters.name: Junction temperature (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum junction temperature.

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range.

parameters.name: ESD, human body model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, human body model. Store as absolute_max.

parameters.name: ESD, charged device model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, charged device model (or field-induced CDM). Store as absolute_max.
